name: Sweet Rice Cakes Stuffed with Yellow Beans
recipeInstructions:
1. Simmer the yellow mung beans in 1 cup of cold water for 20 minutes. Drain and set aside.
2. Mix the dessicated coconut with salt on a plate and set aside.
3. Toast the white sesame seeds over low heat until light brown. Mix evenly with sugar spread on another dish and set aside.
4. In a large bowl, mix rice flour with 1/2 cup of water and knead into dough resembling pie crust consistency.
5. Form the dough into 1 1/2-inch balls, then flatten with your palm to form patties about 1/8 inch thick and 2 inches wide.
6. Cook the rice cakes in a large bowl half full of boiling water for 4 to 5 minutes, or until they start to float.
7. While still hot, coat each side of the cakes with the coconut-salt mixture.
8. Place about 1/4 to 1/2 teaspoon of mung beans into the center of each cake.
9. Fold the edges over and pinch closed to form a half circle.
10. Dip each cake into the sesame seed-sugar mixture, coating both sides well.
11. Serve hot or cold. The cakes will keep in the refrigerator for 2 to 3 days.
